articles.title: Get ready for Google I/O 2026
articles.snippet: Google I/O returns May 19-20. Watch the livestreams for updates on Android, AI, Chrome, and Cloud. Registration is open on the Google I/O website.
articles.ai_summary: Google I/O 2026 is scheduled for May 19-20, with livestreams covering updates on Android, AI, Chrome, and Cloud. Registration is now open.
articles.why_it_matters: Anticipate major announcements in AI and tech from Google at I/O 2026.

articles.title: Turn creative prompts into interactive XR experiences with Gemini
articles.snippet: The Android XR team is using Gemini's Canvas feature to make creating immersive extended reality (XR) experiences more accessible. This allows developers to rapidly prototype interactive 3D environments and models on a Samsung Galaxy XR headset using simple creative prompts.
articles.ai_summary: The Android XR team is leveraging Gemini's Canvas feature to simplify the creation of interactive XR experiences for developers using Samsung Galaxy XR headsets.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances accessibility for developers to create immersive XR environments quickly.

articles.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
articles.snippet: Google has introduced FunctionGemma, a specialized 270M parameter model designed to bring efficient, action-oriented AI experiences directly to mobile devices through on-device function calling. By leveraging Google AI Edge and LiteRT-LM, the model enables complex tasks—such as managing calendars, controlling device hardware, or executing specific game logic in the "Tiny Garden" demo—to be performed entirely offline with high speed and low latency. Available for testing in the Google AI Edge Gal
articles.ai_summary: Google's FunctionGemma model enables efficient on-device function calling for AI tasks, allowing complex operations to be performed offline with low latency.
articles.why_it_matters: Brings powerful AI capabilities directly to mobile devices, enhancing user experience.

articles.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
articles.snippet: Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports a robust ecosystem of third-party tools and integrations. Connect your agents to GitHub, Notion, Hugging Face, and more to build capable, real-world applications.
articles.ai_summary: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports various third-party integrations, allowing developers to enhance their AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion.
articles.why_it_matters: Empowers developers to build more capable and versatile AI applications.

articles.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
articles.snippet: Google I/O 2026 is returning May 19-20 at Shoreline Amphitheatre in Mountain View, CA. But before the keynotes begin, you can get into the spirit of the event with our annual tradition: the save the date puzzle.  This year's experience highlights how AI can empower and accelerate
articles.ai_summary: Google I/O 2026 will feature a 'save the date' puzzle that showcases how AI can enhance event experiences, building excitement ahead of the conference.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights Google's innovative use of AI in event engagement strategies.

articles.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
articles.snippet: While keynotes are available online, Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas offers an irreplaceable in-person experience centered on networking, hands-on problem solving, and the transition to agentic AI. The event features specialized technical tracks covering everything from Gemini multimodal breakthroughs to zero-trust security on Cloud Run, providing developers with the tools to balance individual speed with organizational stability. Beyond formal sessions, the "in-person advantage" lies in over
articles.ai_summary: Google Cloud Next '26 will focus on in-person networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring technical tracks on agentic AI and Gemini breakthroughs.
articles.why_it_matters: Offers developers unique opportunities to engage with cutting-edge cloud technologies directly.

articles.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
articles.snippet: Google has officially launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers significantly faster GPU and NPU acceleration alongside seamless support for PyTorch and JAX. The update also introduces lower-precision data type support for increased efficiency and a commitment to more frequent security and dependency updates across the TensorFlow ecosystem. This transition solidifies LiteRT as Google's primary high-performance framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.
articles.ai_summary: TensorFlow 2.21 introduces LiteRT, a faster framework for deploying GenAI and on-device inference, with improved support for PyTorch and JAX.
articles.why_it_matters: Significantly boosts performance for AI model deployment and enhances developer efficiency.

articles.title: SharePoint: Extending AI in SharePoint using custom skills
articles.snippet: Skills allow extensibility of the SharePoint AI Agent, enabling customizable solutions that adapt to unique business needs. They address gaps in out-of-the-box functionality for unique business processes and enable reusable multi-step workflows. GA date: May CY2026Preview date: April CY2026
articles.ai_summary: SharePoint's AI Agent can now be extended with custom skills, allowing businesses to create tailored solutions and workflows to meet specific needs.
articles.why_it_matters: Facilitates greater customization in business processes through AI integration.

articles.title: Microsoft Teams: Improved organization for muted and meeting chats
articles.snippet: Microsoft Teams automatically organizes your chats with new built in sections that help reduce clutter and keep conversations easy to find. Muted chats (on by default) are grouped together in a dedicated area, while meeting chats (off by default) can be collected into a single section for quick access. You’re always in control, as each section can be turned on or off based on your preferences.GA date: May CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Teams introduces new organization features for chats, grouping muted and meeting chats to reduce clutter and improve accessibility.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances user experience by streamlining communication in Teams.

articles.title: Microsoft Viva: Following feed in Viva Engage
articles.snippet: A new feed type available to users to catch up on posts from people and communities they follow with featured, leadership, and corporate updates.GA date: June CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Viva Engage will launch a new feed type to help users follow posts from communities and leaders, enhancing engagement with corporate updates.
articles.why_it_matters: Improves communication flow and engagement within organizations using Viva.

Use the in-product migration tool in Unified Catalog to migrate classic glossary terms, associated term templates, and asset and column relationships to Unified Catalog
GA date: July CY2026Preview date: April CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft introduced a migration tool for its Unified Catalog, allowing users to transfer classic glossary terms and associated relationships. This tool aims to streamline data management processes for users.

articles.title: Microsoft Teams: Facilitator detects and answers questions
articles.snippet: Facilitator now automatically detects open questions raised during a Teams meeting and offers to help answer them using web search. When a participant asks a question (e.g., “What is an LLM?”) and no one answers, Facilitator posts a prompt offering to find an answer. Participants can select “Yes” to have the agent retrieve and share the information.GA date: June CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Teams has added a feature where the Facilitator detects unanswered questions during meetings and offers to find answers via web search. This aims to improve meeting productivity by providing immediate information.
articles.why_it_matters: Boosts productivity by ensuring questions are addressed in real-time.

articles.title: Baidu’s robotaxis froze in traffic, creating chaos
articles.snippet: Numerous robotaxis operated by Chinese tech giant Baidu froze in a major city on Tuesday, reportedly trapping passengers inside, stranding them on highways, and causing at least one accident in snarled traffic. Police in Wuhan confirmed receiving multiple reports of Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxis stopping in the middle of streets and being unable to move. […]
articles.ai_summary: Baidu's robotaxis malfunctioned in Wuhan, causing traffic chaos and trapping passengers, which raises safety concerns about autonomous vehicle technology. The incident highlights the challenges of deploying robotaxis in urban environments.
articles.why_it_matters: Underscores the risks and challenges of implementing autonomous vehicles in real-world scenarios.

articles.title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
articles.snippet: The AI recruiting startup confirmed a security incident after an extortion hacking crew took credit for stealing data from the company's systems.
articles.ai_summary: Mercor, an AI recruiting startup, confirmed it suffered a cyberattack linked to the compromise of the open-source LiteLLM project, resulting in data theft. This incident highlights vulnerabilities in AI-related projects and the risks associated with open-source software.

articles.title: Manifold topological deep learning for biomedical data
articles.snippet: The study introduces manifold topological deep learning (MTDL) that extends topological deep learning to data on differentiable manifolds through de Rham-Hodge theory. MTDL outperforms competing methods on various biomedical imaging benchmarks.
articles.ai_summary: A new study introduces manifold topological deep learning (MTDL), which enhances topological deep learning techniques for biomedical data. MTDL has shown superior performance on various biomedical imaging benchmarks, indicating its potential in healthcare applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Improves AI methods in biomedical research, potentially advancing healthcare technology.

articles.title: Quantifying uncertainty in protein representations across models and tasks
articles.snippet: A model-agnostic empirical framework is proposed to measure the uncertainty associated with protein embeddings and to assess the biological relevance of these embeddings in order to improve model reliability and performance on downstream tasks.
articles.ai_summary: A model-agnostic framework has been proposed to quantify uncertainty in protein representations across different models and tasks. This framework aims to enhance the reliability and performance of models in biological applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Addresses a critical aspect of AI in biology, improving model accuracy and trustworthiness.

articles.title: Lenovo Yoga Mini i Surfaces with Intel Panther Lake and 32 GB RAM
articles.snippet: Before its global rollout, Lenovo first launched the Yoga Mini i mini PC in China, a few months after its introduction at CES 2026. The Yoga Mini i is built around the Intel Panther Lake platform, with configurations listed up to a Core Ultra X7 385H processo…
articles.ai_summary: Lenovo has launched the Yoga Mini i mini PC in China, featuring Intel's Panther Lake platform and up to 32 GB RAM, ahead of its global rollout.
articles.why_it_matters: Showcases Lenovo's innovation in compact computing with advanced hardware.

articles.title: Microsoft closes worst quarter on Wall Street since 2008 on AI concerns: 'Redmond is in a pickle'
articles.snippet: With Microsoft losing almost a quarter of its value this year, investors have reset the company's earnings multiple to the lowest since late 2022.
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft's stock has plummeted nearly 25% this year, marking its worst quarterly performance since 2008, largely due to investor concerns over AI strategies.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights significant investor skepticism about Microsoft's AI direction and financial health.

articles.title: California Tightens AI Contract Rules as Fight With Trump Admin Grows
articles.snippet: Governor Gavin Newsom orders stronger safeguards for AI companies seeking California contracts, escalating tensions with the Trump administration over national AI regulation.
articles.ai_summary: California's Governor Gavin Newsom has implemented stricter regulations for AI companies seeking state contracts, intensifying a conflict with the Trump administration over AI governance.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals increasing state-level scrutiny and regulation of AI technologies.

articles.title: Iran threatens imminent attacks on US tech companies in the Middle East
articles.snippet: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), a branch of Iran's armed forces, has threatened to target US tech companies' operations in the Middle East. It told employees of 18 companies — including Apple, Google, Meta and NVIDIA — “to leave their workplaces…
articles.ai_summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has threatened imminent attacks on US tech companies operating in the Middle East, warning employees to leave workplaces.
articles.why_it_matters: Raises concerns about geopolitical risks for tech companies in volatile regions.

articles.title: Apple adds Apple TV and iPad mini models to ‘obsolete’ products list

 more…

articles.title: Apple Intelligence briefly goes live in China without approval, raising the spectre of regulatory penalties
articles.snippet: n the small hours of Tuesday morning, something unexpected appeared on iPhones across mainland China. Apple Intelligence, the suite of AI-powered tools that the company has spent nearly two years trying to bring to its largest market outside the US, flickered…
articles.ai_summary: Apple Intelligence briefly launched in China without regulatory approval, raising concerns about potential penalties and compliance issues.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the challenges tech companies face in navigating regulatory environments.

articles.title: A16z-backed Yupp AI shuts down operations; here is why
articles.snippet: Launched for public use in June last year, Yupp was a free-to-use platform that provided users with response options generated by over 500 generative AI models, including OpenAI's ChatGPT, Anthropic's Claude, Google's Gemini, and models from Mistral. In addit…
articles.ai_summary: Yupp AI, a platform that offered generative AI response options, has shut down just months after its launch due to operational challenges. This closure highlights the difficulties startups face in the competitive AI landscape.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals the volatility and challenges in the AI startup ecosystem.

articles.ai_summary: The source code for Claude AI was leaked, causing a stir in the developer community. This incident raises concerns about legal repercussions and the security of proprietary AI technologies.
articles.why_it_matters: Exposes vulnerabilities in AI security and intellectual property protection.

articles.title: AI’s ability to see ‘mirages’ shows how alien machine brains really are
articles.snippet: AI models can analyze images that don't exist, new research shows, raising big questions about how they work and well they will perform in real world settings
articles.ai_summary: New research reveals that AI models can identify and analyze non-existent images, prompting questions about their operational mechanisms and real-world applicability. This discovery could reshape our understanding of AI capabilities.
articles.why_it_matters: Challenges existing perceptions of AI's cognitive abilities and reliability.

articles.title: cognity-ai added to PyPI

articles.ai_summary: Cognity-ai has been added to the Python Package Index (PyPI), offering a modular library for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) that supports various LLMs and data formats. This addition enhances accessibility for developers.
articles.why_it_matters: Facilitates easier integration of advanced AI capabilities into applications.

articles.title: Microsoft Copilot Was Injecting Ads Into GitHub Pulls Requests And Merges

articles.title: WhatsApp malware campaign delivers VBS payloads and MSI backdoors
articles.snippet:
A malware campaign uses WhatsApp messages to deliver VBS scripts that initiate a multi-stage infection chain. The attack leverages renamed Windows tools and cloud-hosted payloads to install MSI backdoors and maintain persistent access to compromised systems.
…
articles.ai_summary: A new malware campaign is exploiting WhatsApp to deliver VBS scripts that install backdoors on systems. This multi-stage attack poses significant security risks to users.
articles.why_it_matters: Raises awareness about evolving cybersecurity threats and the importance of vigilance.

articles.title: This is Microsoft's new "Copilot Cowork": An experiment with Anthropic's Claude AI models that plans and delegates your work

articles.ai_summary: Microsoft has launched 'Copilot Cowork,' an experimental tool utilizing Anthropic's Claude AI models to assist users in planning and delegating tasks. This aims to enhance productivity.
articles.why_it_matters: Showcases the integration of AI in workplace tools to improve task management.
